quizExercises.sentence: She introduced a fun activity at the start of the session to ___ with the new participants.
quizExercises.explanation: 'Break the ice' means to ease social tension or awkwardness at the start of a group interaction.

quizExercises.question: Which situation best describes 'burning the midnight oil'?
options: Finishing work early and leaving the office before anyone else
Working late into the night to meet a deadline
Taking a long lunch break during the working day
quizExercises.explanation: 'Burn the midnight oil' means to work or study very late at night.

quizExercises.sentence: I'm not against overtime, but I ___ at being asked to cancel my holiday.
quizExercises.explanation: 'Draw the line' means to set a personal limit on what you will accept.

quizExercises.question: If two people 'don't see eye to eye', what is true?
options: They have the same opinion on everything
They have not met each other in a long time
quizExercises.explanation: 'See eye to eye' means to agree. 'Not seeing eye to eye' means disagreeing.

quizExercises.sentence: The new product launch ___ of projections – they only sold half the expected units.
quizExercises.explanation: 'Fall short' means to fail to reach the expected level or standard.

quizExercises.idiom: bury your head in the sand
quizExercises.question: A company knows its product has a safety flaw but ignores complaints and continues selling it. This is an example of what?
options: Burying their head in the sand
quizExercises.explanation: 'Bury your head in the sand' means to deliberately ignore a serious problem.

quizExercises.sentence: He said he hated social media, but after he got a big following, he completely ___.
options: got the wrong end of the stick
got on like a house on fire
quizExercises.explanation: 'Change your tune' means to adopt a very different opinion, often when circumstances benefit you.

quizExercises.question: Which scenario best illustrates 'in the nick of time'?
options: You arrive at the cinema thirty minutes early
You arrive just as the doors are about to close
You miss the film and have to reschedule
quizExercises.explanation: 'In the nick of time' means arriving or acting at the very last possible moment.

quizExercises.sentence: I'd take on a new challenge ___. I love trying new things.
options: at the drop of a hat
quizExercises.correct: at the drop of a hat
quizExercises.explanation: 'At the drop of a hat' means immediately, without hesitation.

quizExercises.question: Why is it important for a team to be 'on the same page' before starting a project?
options: So that everyone reads the same book
So that all members share the same understanding of goals and expectations
So that the project is finished as quickly as possible
quizExercises.explanation: 'On the same page' means everyone has the same understanding, which prevents miscommunication.
